name: Sealtest Whipping Cream 35%
comments: From nutrition facts table: https://www.sealtest.ca/en/products/creams/whipping-cream-35. The label lists 1g of carbohydrates and 0g of sugars. There shouldn't be any other carbohydrates other than lactose (there are trace amounts), so we take the middle value of 0.5g sugars - this is consistent with the standard ~60/40 ratio of lactose to proteins in MSNF - [constants::composition::STD_LACTOSE_IN_MSNF], [constants::composition::STD_PROTEIN_IN_MSNF].

name: Carnation Fat Free Evaporated Milk
comments: From nutrition facts table: https://www.carnationmilk.ca/en/products/fat-free-skim. Serving size is 15ml at 1.075 g/ml [constants::density::EVAPORATED_MILK].

name: Carnation 2% Evaporated Partly Skimmed Milk
comments: From nutrition facts table: https://www.carnationmilk.ca/en/products/2-partly-skimmed. Serving size is 15ml at 1.075 g/ml [constants::density::EVAPORATED_MILK]. The label lists 2g of carbohydrates and 1g of sugars. There shouldn't be any other carbohydrates other than lactose (there are trace amounts), so we take the middle value of 1.5g sugars - this is consistent with the standard ~60/40 ratio of lactose to proteins in MSNF - [constants::composition::STD_LACTOSE_IN_MSNF], [constants::composition::STD_PROTEIN_IN_MSNF].

name: Carnation Evaporated Milk
comments: From nutrition facts table: https://www.carnationmilk.ca/en/products/evaporated-milk. Serving size is 15ml at 1.075 g/ml [constants::density::EVAPORATED_MILK]. The label lists 2g of carbohydrates and 1g of sugars. There shouldn't be any other carbohydrates other than lactose (there are trace amounts), so we take the middle value of 1.5g sugars - this is consistent with the standard ~60/40 ratio of lactose to proteins in MSNF - [constants::composition::STD_LACTOSE_IN_MSNF], [constants::composition::STD_PROTEIN_IN_MSNF].

name: USDA Sweetened Condensed Milk
comments: USDA FoodData Central - Survey (FNDDS) - Milk, condensed, sweetened: https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/2705402/nutrients. Lactose amount from USDA FoodData Central - Foundation - Sweetened condensed milk: https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/2758990/nutrients.

name: Eagle Brand Original Sweetened Condensed Milk
comments: From nutrition facts table: https://www.eaglebrand.ca/en/products/original. Serving size is 15ml at 1.3 g/ml [constants::density::SWEETENED_CONDENSED_MILK]. The label does not specify lactose content, so it is assumed as 46% of total weight (Sweetened condensed milk, 2026)[^117], (Moros, 1985)[^51].

name: Eagle Brand Low Fat Sweetened Condensed Milk
comments: From nutrition facts table: https://www.eaglebrand.ca/en/products/low-fat. Serving size is 15ml at 1.3 g/ml [constants::density::SWEETENED_CONDENSED_MILK]. The label does not specify lactose content, so it is assumed as 46% of total weight (Sweetened condensed milk, 2026)[^117], (Moros, 1985)[^51].

name: Eagle Brand Dulce de Leche Caramel Flavoured Sauce
comments: From nutrition facts table: https://www.eaglebrand.ca/en/products/dulce-de-leche. Serving size is 15ml at 1.3 g/ml [constants::density::SWEETENED_CONDENSED_MILK]. The label specifies 11g of carbohydrates and 10g of sugars, which is expected since some sugars should have caramelized into more complex carbohydrates. We just leave sugars as 10g and ignore the 1g. The label does not specify lactose content, so it is assumed as 82% of total sugars (Sweetened condensed milk, 2026)[^117], (Moros, 1985)[^51].
